SUMMARY:
SUSTAINABLE INVESTING | Major Sustainability Sustainable Investing According toUS Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), sustainable finance, also called ESG finance, refers to investments that consider environmental, social, and/or governance factors. There are many different approaches to sustainable investing. The most commonly used sustainable investment strategies include: negative screening, positive screening, ESG integration, impact investing, and more. The Six Main Types of Sustainable Investing The construction of a portfolio of investments to match an impact theme (e.g. gender equity, clean water) or an established indices, such as MSCI or the Dow Jones Sustainability Index, that provides rankings and ratings of environmentally and socially responsible companies. #### FINANCE & SUSTAINABILITY

Sustainable Investing
---------------------

According to**[US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)](https://www.investor.gov/introduction-investing/general-resources/news-alerts/alerts-bulletins/investor-bulletins-1)**, sustainable finance, also called _ESG_ finance, refers to investments that consider environmental, social, and/or governance factors. Investors that engage with sustainable investing tend to invest through the lens of _financial return + social return = total return._

There are many different approaches to sustainable investing. The most commonly used sustainable investment strategies include: negative screening, positive screening, ESG integration, impact investing, and more.

Below is a brief introduction of each of the main types of sustainable investing approaches. To read more about how sustainable and ESG investing evolved [check out this timeline from the CFA Institute’s The Rise of ESG Investing](https://interactive.cfainstitute.org/ESG-guide/esg-past-and-present-238UB-188148.html).

#### The Six Main Types of Sustainable Investing

The information provided below comes from the work of the [Summit Consulting](https://www.summitllc.us/) report “Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) Investment Tools: A Review of the Current Field” (2017). The examples given should not be understood as investment advice or the preferences of the site authors but are provided only so the reader has something concrete that illustrates the concept.

![Image 2: Screen Shot 2022-03-28 at 4.22.57 PM](https://majorsustainability.smeal.psu.edu/files/2020/07/Screen-Shot-2022-03-28-at-4.22.57-PM.png)

**Negative screening**

Investments are determined by what undesirable social and/or environmental outcomes the investor wants to exclude. Exclusion criteria can be include product categories (e.g. weapons, tobacco), activities (e.g. animal testing) or practices (e.g. labor rights violations, corruption).

Example: The [Change Finance U.S. Large Cap Fossil Fuel Free ETF](https://www.axsinvestments.com/axs-change-finance-esg-etf/#:~:text=and%20shareholder%20rights.-,Change%20Finance%20Diversified%20Impact%20U.S.%20Large%20Cap%20Fossil%20Fuel%20Free,diverse%20set%20of%20ESG%20standards.)

![Image 3: tim-mossholder-jEOAOVq39Z4-unsplash](https://majorsustainability.smeal.psu.edu/files/2020/07/tim-mossholder-jEOAOVq39Z4-unsplash.jpg)

**Positive screening**

Rather than determing investments based on what you DON’T want (negative screening), positive screening selects investments based on what you DO want. Relative to industry peers, investments are selected based on prioritized ESG factors and desired social and/or environmental outcomes.

Example: [Harlem Capital Partners](https://harlem.capital/)

![Image 4: vardan-papikyan-DnXqvmS0eXM-unsplash](https://majorsustainability.smeal.psu.edu/files/2020/07/vardan-papikyan-DnXqvmS0eXM-unsplash.jpg)

**ESG Integration**

The inclusion of ESG risks and opportunities into traditional financial analysis and investment decisions. ESG outcomes don’t determine outcomes (like in Positive Screening or Impact Investing) but ESG data and analysis is part of the due diligence process.

Example: [Pax Global Environmental Markets Fund](https://impaxam.com/products/thematic-equities/leaders-strategy/pax-global-environmental-markets-fund/)

![Image 5: mauro-gigli-1JX4J_kq7sU-unsplash](https://majorsustainability.smeal.psu.edu/files/2020/07/mauro-gigli-1JX4J_kq7sU-unsplash.jpg)

**Impact Investing**

The generation of positive social and environmental outcomes is what drives this approach to sustainable finance. Financial returns are required but there is greater acceptance of below market returns as long as the desired impact is perceived to be realized.

Example: [W.K. Kellogg Foundation’s Mission Driven Investment (MDI)](https://www.wkkf.org/what-we-do/mission-driven-investments)

![Image 6: trinh-le-nguyen-ai-sWnVuda_pks-unsplash](https://majorsustainability.smeal.psu.edu/files/2020/07/trinh-le-nguyen-ai-sWnVuda_pks-unsplash.jpg)

**Thematic and Index Based**

The construction of a portfolio of investments to match an impact theme (e.g. gender equity, clean water) or an established indices, such as MSCI or the Dow Jones Sustainability Index, that provides rankings and ratings of environmentally and socially responsible companies.

Example: [Calvert U.S. Large Cap Core Responsible Index Fund](https://www.calvert.com/Calvert-US-Large-Cap-Core-Responsible-Index-Fund-CISIX.php)

![Image 7: cytonn-photography-n95VMLxqM2I-unsplash](https://majorsustainability.smeal.psu.edu/files/2020/07/cytonn-photography-n95VMLxqM2I-unsplash.jpg)

**Corporate engagement and activism**

The direct engagement of corporations through work to influence and encourage the board of directors to adopt sustainability/ESG practices. This includes the work of shareholder activists whose proposals and petitions pushing for work on climate change, diversity and labor rights protections has been on the rise ([Harvard Law School](https://corpgov.law.harvard.edu/2021/11/29/recent-shareholder-activism-trends/)).

Example: [Engine N0. 1 and Exxon](https://www.reuters.com/business/little-engine-no-1-beat-exxon-with-just-125-mln-sources-2021-06-29/)

#### The Rise of Sustainable Investing

According to the [Forum for Sustainable and Responsible Investment (USSIF)](https://www.ussif.org/index.asp), sustainable investing is involved in one out of every three dollars that is professionally invested in the U.S. This equates to around $17 trillion in total assets invested using sustainable investing principles.

Globally, nearly 5,000 investment managers, asset owners, financial services providers are [Principles for Responsible Investment (PRI)](https://www.unpri.org/) signatories ([what are they signing onto?](https://www.unpri.org/about-us/what-are-the-principles-for-responsible-investment)).

Finally, the wise finance student and professional recognizes that the rise of sustainable/ESG investing faces criticism around the quality of data, trustworthiness of claims of impact, and an overall sense that ESG investing perhaps wasn’t even “designed to save the planet” in the first place ([Harvard Business Review](https://hbr.org/2022/08/esg-investing-isnt-designed-to-save-the-planet)).

[![Image 8: PRI Growth 2006-2021](https://majorsustainability.smeal.psu.edu/files/2020/07/pri-sigs.png)](https://www.unpri.org/)

Learn about more sustainability concepts within this major.

--- SOURCE 4: Sustainability and Responsible Investment ---
URL: https://www.theia.org/campaigns/sustainability-and-responsible-investment

SUMMARY:
Sustainability and Responsible Investment. Explores how asset managers integrate ESG factors into investment decisions and stewardship to drive long-term value.

FULL CONTENT:
![Investment Association logo](/sites/default/files/IA%20website%20resized%20logo_0.png)

## Main navigation

# Sustainability and Responsible Investment

Explores how asset managers integrate ESG factors into investment decisions and stewardship to drive long-term value.

## Breadcrumb

![Investment Association logo](/sites/default/files/IA%20website%20resized%20logo_0.png)

## **Sustainability and the investment management industry**

As stewards of our clients’ assets, investment managers have taken responsible investment approaches for many years – approaches that consider environmental, social, and governance (ESG) criteria in their investment analysis and decisions. The aim: to achieve better long-term returns for clients.

There has never been a greater focus on how well investment management firms deliver for their customers, and for the wider economy.

We’re seeing three major drivers in sustainability for investment management:

## **The Investment Association’s industry-led initiatives**

At the IA, we’re working closely with our member firms to promote responsible investment. Above all, we’re helping firms think about their wider role in society and the environment.

Our work has focused on the following:

## **The FCA’s Sustainability Disclosure Requirements (SDR) and Investment Labels**

In November 2023, the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) published its Policy Statement ([PS23/16](https://www.fca.org.uk/publication/policy/ps23-16.pdf "Policy Statement (PS23/16)")) on Sustainability Disclosure Requirements (SDR) and investment labels. The Policy Statement sets out the FCA’s final rules on anti-greenwashing, a new labelling regime, naming and marketing rules, product and entity level disclosures, as well as distributor obligations.

The IA is running numerous workstreams across the breadth of the FCA's rules on SDR and investment labels. To view our latest publications, please click the button below.

## Member area

Members can visit the following pages to find out more about the IA's work on sustainability and responsible investment:

--- SOURCE 5: What is responsible investment? ---
URL: https://www.unpri.org/introductory-guides-to-responsible-investment/what-is-responsible-investment/4780.article

SUMMARY:
Responsible investment involves considering environmental, social and governance (ESG) issues when making investment decisions and influencing companies or assets (known as active ownership or stewardship). ESG issues that investors can consider when investing responsibly include: Image 8: Graphic showing the drivers of responsible investment: financial materiality; client demand; policy and regulation; fiduciary duties; sustainability outcomes; Principles for Responsible Investment PRI reporting data shows that responsible investment practices are more advanced in some asset classes than in others. Asset owners can use the information in these disclosures to assess the ESG incorporation and stewardship practices, and actions on sustainability outcomes, taken by their investment managers. Climate change is a high-priority ESG issue for investors, particularly asset owners, as outlined in the PRI’s analysis of asset owner signatories’ responsible investment practices.

FULL CONTENT:
Published Time: 2023-01-31

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://financial.dapodik.co.id/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/Exploring-sustainable-investing-Aligning-financial-goals-with-values-LJg-oPe.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A diagram showing sustainable IT investment strategies with icons and descriptions.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://districtcapitalmanagement.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/04/3-popular-sustainable-investing-strategies-.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The content outlines three popular sustainable investing strategies: Green ETFs, which target environmentally-friendly companies; Sustainability Index Funds, focusing on businesses screened for ESG criteria; and Actively Managed Funds, selecting socially responsible companies for higher potential returns.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://www.wallstreetmojo.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Sustainable-Finance.png
DESCRIPTION: A flowchart outlines the process of sustainable finance, emphasizing that investment decisions are made by considering ESG factors, which ultimately leads to long-term investments.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://img.freepik.com/premium-photo/responsible-investment-strategies-sustainable-finance-disclosure-corporate-esg-performance_38013-76210.jpg
DESCRIPTION: Young plants are growing from stacks of coins, symbolizing responsible investment strategies for sustainable finance.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://treasuryxl.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Sustainable-Finance-Framework-705x705.png
DESCRIPTION: A circular flow chart outlines the components of a Sustainable Finance Framework, including key elements such as "Use of Proceeds Statement," "Project Evaluation & Selection," "Management of Loan Proceeds," "Assurance," "Reporting," and "Framework Enhancement."

enriched_points.expanded_content: Promote investment strategies that prioritize environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors, aligning financial goals with sustainable practices to drive positive societal impact.
Support initiatives that enhance disclosure and reporting on ESG metrics, ensuring stakeholders have access to reliable information to make informed investment decisions.
Engage with policymakers to foster regulations that promote sustainable finance, reduce greenwashing, and establish clear standards for investment practices within the industry.
